event: The Virgin and Child with the infant Saint John and his parents
symbols: lamb at the children's feet — Christ as the Lamb of God, prefiguring his sacrificial death
birdcage with bird — the soul's imprisonment in the flesh, or the sacrifice to be offered at the Temple purification
infant John reaching toward Jesus — the Baptist's future role of pointing to Christ
elderly couple — Zechariah and Elizabeth, the miraculous parents of John
description: A warm, intimate domestic scene showing five figures gathered around a wicker chair. An elderly bearded man (Zechariah) and elderly woman in a white headcovering (Elizabeth) stand at the left, looking down at two infants. One infant reaches toward a birdcage held between the groups. A young woman in a red garment (Mary) holds the other infant on the right, seated in a large wicker chair. A small white lamb sits at the children's feet. A dark red curtain drapes behind the left figures. The palette is warm with rich reds, browns, and golden flesh tones.
theological_description: This painting brings together the two holy families whose destinies are intertwined by divine plan: Mary with the infant Jesus and Elizabeth and Zechariah with the infant John. The scene extends the narrative of the Visitation (Luke 1:39-45), when Elizabeth's child leaped in the womb at Mary's greeting, into a tender domestic encounter between the two children. The lamb at their feet is the defining symbol: John will grow up to proclaim Jesus as 'the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world' (John 1:29). The infant John reaching toward the birdcage or toward Jesus enacts in miniature the Baptist's entire prophetic mission — pointing others to Christ. Elizabeth and Zechariah represent the faithful remnant of Israel, the righteous who 'walked blamelessly in all the commandments' (Luke 1:6), and from whose aged bodies God brought forth the forerunner, demonstrating that 'nothing is impossible with God' (Luke 1:37). The domestic warmth of the scene grounds the Incarnation in ordinary human life — God enters the world through families, through the bonds of kinship and love.

scripture_refs.reason: The Visitation — the meeting of the two mothers and their unborn children that this scene extends
Behold the Lamb of God — the lamb at the children's feet prefiguring John's proclamation
The introduction of Zechariah and Elizabeth, the elderly righteous couple seen here
Elizabeth's miraculous conception in old age, demonstrating that nothing is impossible with God
Zechariah's prophecy about John — you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways

_meta.title: The Virgin and Child with St John and His Parents
